Punjab braces for rain, hailstorm as heatwave likely to ease
Babushahi Bureau
Chandigarh, May 27, 2026: Residents across Punjab may finally get relief from the scorching summer heat as the Meteorological Department has issued an alert for heavy rain, thunderstorms and hailstorms in several parts of the state over the next two days.
According to the latest weather forecast, conditions are expected to change significantly from Thursday onward, with cloudy skies likely to prevail across most districts from early morning. Strong winds, dust storms and isolated hailstorms are also expected in many areas.
Officials said the weather system could bring down temperatures by nearly 4 to 5 degrees Celsius, offering much-needed respite from the intense heatwave conditions that recently pushed temperatures close to 45 degrees Celsius in parts of the state.
The weather department has issued a special alert for districts including Mohali, Patiala, Rupnagar, Hoshiarpur and Gurdaspur, where rainfall and cloudy weather are likely to remain active.
While the expected change in weather is set to provide relief from the blistering heat, authorities have also advised residents to remain cautious due to the possibility of strong winds and hail activity in vulnerable areas.

articles.title: Punjab: Agriculture Department issues advisory to protect sugarcane crop from heatwave - ChiniMandi
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Punjab
articles.full_text:
Amritsar: With intense heatwave conditions continuing across the region, the Department of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare has issued an advisory urging sugarcane farmer to adopt preventive measures to protect their crop and avoid production losses.
The advisory, released by the Cane Commissioner and the Sugarcane Research and Development Board, stresses the importance of regular irrigation to maintain adequate soil moisture. Farmers have been advised to irrigate sugarcane fields every 10 to 15 days to prevent moisture stress caused by extreme temperatures, The Tribune reported.
